statement: The v0 normalized quota-dimension enum should support `requests`, `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `total_tokens`, and optional `batch_enqueued_input_tokens`; providers select the subset they enforce, while `cached_input_tokens`, `thinking_tokens`, and `service_tier` remain tracked usage fields rather than independently enforced shared-bucket dimensions.
rationale: A portable engine needs one honest superset for Anthropic-style split token quotas, Gemini-style input TPM, and OpenAI-style shared token TPM. Richer usage details remain important, but they do not all need independent shared-bucket enforcement in the first rewrite.
keyAssumptions: The first rewrite prioritizes cross-worker burst correctness over complete modeling of every provider quota dimension
Daily or provider-specific long-horizon quotas can remain future policy extensions without breaking the core architecture
validationCriteria: The supported enum is sufficient for OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini sync support in v0
Tracked-only usage fields still preserve analytics and replay value without forcing extra Upstash buckets
The registry can grow later without schema churn in core workflow logic
critiquePoints: Some real workloads may eventually need separate enforcement for cached or thinking tokens
Batch queue semantics may still vary enough that `batch_enqueued_input_tokens` needs provider-specific refinement
questions: Should daily limits become first-class dimensions in v1 if spend controls become operationally important?
